task_completion.description: Load the 'countries_cleaning.shp' dataset into gvSIG Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Desktop application is open and 'countries_cleaning' is visible in the Table of Contents (TOC) or the Project Manager window.

task_completion.description: Open the attribute table for the loaded shapefile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An attribute table window is open for the 'countries_cleaning' layer, displaying data columns including the 'POP_EST' field.

task_completion.description: Enable editing mode for the shapefile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer is in editing mode, indicated by active editing tools in the toolbar, or the layer name highlighted/marked in red in the TOC.

task_completion.description: Identify and select the features where 'POP_EST' equals -99.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rows in the attribute table with '-99' in the 'POP_EST' column are highlighted (selected), or a 'Select by Attributes' / Filter dialog is visible querying for 'POP_EST = -99'.
The agent clicks the 'Delete row' / 'Delete selected features' button, or the previously selected rows with '-99' disappear from the attribute table.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the shapefile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'Finish Editing' and confirms saving changes, or clicks a 'Save' button specifically for the layer edits, ensuring the shapefile is permanently updated.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG menus, toolbars, and attribute tables rather than terminal windows.

integrity.description: The agent did not use command-line tools or scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'ogr2ogr', 'qgis_process', or Python scripts (e.g., geopandas) being used to filter and save the shapefile.

integrity.description: The agent actually deleted the records and saved the file, rather than just applying a visual filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: The editing tools were used to delete rows and save the file, rather than just using a display filter or symbology rule to hide the -99 values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/countries_cleaning.shp'. The invalid value to identify and remove is exactly '-99' in the 'POP_EST' field. There are exactly 15 invalid records containing this value that must be deleted.
